behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Needs a real HTTP error-path contract check (401) plus verification that no enqueue side effect happens. Unit tests of HMAC logic miss handler/wiring failures between HTTP layer, raw-body extraction, and queue forwarding. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML dependency, no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Send a real HTTP webhook request with a tampered body/signature pair to the running service; assert 401 response, then read back queue state and confirm no new event was persisted.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: Requires proving ordering between an external response and a persistent side effect via round-trip readback, which is a System triad trigger. Unit/integration mocks can falsely pass without proving real persistence timing. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML output dependence and no multi-step agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Post a valid webhook over real HTTP and instrument timing/checkpoints so the test verifies queue persistence is observable before the 200 response is observed by the client.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is orchestration between handler, queue handoff, and downstream worker completion state; this can be exposed with in-process wiring and controlled slow downstream promises. Rejected System: behavior text does not require validating a specific external response contract shape beyond early return semantics, and no explicit real-OS/network-only failure is stated. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler + queue + downstream processor in-process; make downstream processing block on a latch, call handler, assert response is produced before latch release, then release latch and assert eventual processing completion.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test is explicitly multi-module and timing/orchestration focused, not a single-function assertion.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: This is a real wall-clock SLO under concurrency and backlog pressure; scheduler/event-loop/runtime behavior can differ from mocks. Needs real HTTP timing observation under concurrent load, which is System-level infrastructure validation. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service, create slow concurrent downstream load from earlier events, then send a new valid webhook via real HTTP and assert end-to-end client-observed latency is under 3 seconds.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module handoff/state level (dedupe store/constraint + enqueue path), not just pure function logic. A real local persistence layer (DB/filesystem) is enough; no explicit requirement for real network/subprocess boundary. Rejected System: behavior does not explicitly require external HTTP contract or external boundary round-trip beyond internal persistence correctness. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model usage.
behaviors.test_description: Use real dedupe + persistence modules together, submit the same event ID twice through the handler path, and assert only one queue record exists after both attempts.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Explicit external error-path contract (status plus header) must be validated, which matches the System triad. Unit/integration with mocked response objects can miss real HTTP serialization/header behavior. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML dependence or multi-step agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Force real queue-full condition, send webhook over real HTTP, and assert response is 503 with a valid Retry-After header value in the actual network response.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: Requires real queue implementation + persistence behavior across modules; unit tests with fake queue can miss real limit enforcement. No explicit requirement for network/subprocess/external HTTP contract, so Integration is sufficient. Rejected System: behavior text focuses on internal persistent queue semantics, not external boundary contract checks.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue backend with a small capacity, enqueue up to the limit, then attempt one more enqueue and assert the real backend rejects it and state remains at configured max.
